title: Fancy Fried Greek Pasta Casserole
description: A crispy, savory Greek dish featuring jerked ground beef and deep-fry tofu, perfect for a high-protein meal. This show-off pasta casserole is a great option for special occasions.
instructions: Cook the Greek pasta according to the package instructions and set aside.
In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of unsalted butter over medium-high heat. Add the diced red onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the ground beef to the skillet and cook until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the jerk seasoning to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the tofu to the skillet and cook until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked pasta, ground beef mixture, and feta cheese. Mix well to combine.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs and a pinch of salt. Add the shredded parmesan cheese and mix until well combined.
Add the egg mixture to the pasta mixture and stir until well combined.
Transfer the pasta mixture to a deep frying pan or a deep fryer and fry until golden brown and crispy, about 3-4 minutes.
Remove the casserole from the oil and place it on a paper towel-lined plate to drain any excess oil.
Transfer the casserole to a serving dish and top with kalamata olives and a sprinkle of oregano.
Sprinkle with thyme and parmesan cheese before serving, if desired.
